steps.description: Type the text "bot app with sso enable" into the search/filter input field located above the sample list in the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension within Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Create" button for the "Bot App with SSO Enabled" sample project in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit Samples tab within Visual Studio Code.
And input ${{var:app_name:sso-bot}} into the run time variable.
Use the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+P to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code, within the Explorer view of the BOT-SSO project workspace.
Type 'view: show microsoft' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette input field at the top center of the application window.
Press the Enter key to select the "View: Show Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" command from the Visual Studio Code command palette.
Click the "Sign in to Microsoft 365" button in the ACCOUNTS section of the MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT sidebar within Visual Studio Code, targeting the leftmost icon next to the label.
Click the "Sign in" button in the Microsoft 365 developer sandbox prompt at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Type '${{env:MS_AZURE_ACCOUNT_NAME}}' into the email or username input field on the Microsoft account sign-in page displayed in a Chrome browser.
Click the "Next" button on the Microsoft sign-in page after entering the email address "fxtest02@microsoft.com" in the login.microsoftonline.com web interface.
Type text '${{secret:MS_AZURE_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}}' into the Password field on the Microsoft sign-in page for user fxtest02@microsoft.com in a Chrome browser.
Click the "Sign in" button on the Microsoft account password entry page in the Chrome browser.
Click the red cross (window close button) in the top-right corner of the "M365 Account - Sign In" Google Chrome window displaying the Visual Studio Code sign-in confirmation page.
Click the "Sign in to Azure" link under the ACCOUNTS section of the MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT pane in Visual Studio Code to initiate Azure authentication.
Click the "Sign in" button on the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit authentication prompt in Visual Studio Code to begin Azure sign-in.
Click the "Allow" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sign-in permissions dialog within Visual Studio Code to grant access using Microsoft.
Click the vertical ellipsis (three dots) menu next to the listed Microsoft account (fxstest02@microsoft.com) in the "Pick an account" dialog on the Microsoft login page in Google Chrome.
Click the primary account selection row for '${{env:MS_AZURE_ACCOUNT_NAME}}' in the Microsoft login "Pick an account" dialog on login.microsoftonline.com.
Type text '${{secret:MS_AZURE_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}}' into the Password field on the Microsoft login page for user '${{env:MS_AZURE_ACCOUNT_NAME}}'.
Click the "Sign in" button on the Microsoft login page after entering the password.
Click the "Close" button (red 'X') at the top-right corner of the Chrome browser window displaying the Visual Studio Code sign-in confirmation page.
Press the F5 key in Visual Studio Code's "MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT" panel to trigger the "Preview Your App" action under the DEVELOPMENT section.
@assertion Unable to apply this subscription.
